channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Moscow warns the US over allowing Ukraine to hit Russian soil with longer-range weapons
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The Kremlin warned Monday that President Joe Biden’s decision to let Ukraine strike targets inside Russia with U.S.-supplied longer-range missiles adds “fuel to the fire” of the war and would escalate international tensions even higher.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Negotiators are urged to get down to business as climate talks in Baku enter second week
item.description: BAKU, Azerbaijan (AP) — United Nations climate talks resumed Monday with negotiators urged to make progress on a stalled-out deal that could see developing countries get more money to spend on clean energy and adapting to climate-charged weather extremes. U.N.
item.dc:creator: SIBI ARASU, MELINA WALLING and SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Typhoon Man-yi leaves 7 dead in Philippines and worsens crisis from back-to-back storms
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Typhoon Man-yi left at least seven people dead in a landslide, destroyed houses and displaced large numbers of villagers before blowing away from the northern Philippines, worsening the crisis wreaked by multiple back-to-back storms, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Teenage Buddhist lama celebrates last birthday in the U.S. before joining monastery in the Himalayas
item.description: ISANTI, Minn. (AP) — The youthful Buddhist lama sat on a throne near an altar decorated with flowers, fruits and golden statues of the Buddha, watching the celebrations of his 18th birthday in silence, with a faint smile.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Protesters in Georgia's capital set up a tent camp on the main street and call for new elections
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Demonstrators in the capital of Georgia have set up tents on one of the city's main thoroughfares and vowed Monday to stay there around the clock to demand new parliamentary elections in the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Children and parents among 8 killed in Israeli strikes in Gaza, officials say
item.description: Palestinian officials say Israeli strikes in the Gaza Strip killed eight people, including two children aged 7 and 9 and their parents.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia launches one of its fiercest missile and drone attacks at Ukraine's infrastructure
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian strike on a nine-story building in the city of Sumy in northern Ukraine killed eight people and wounded dozens, an official said Sunday, as Russia launched a massive drone and missile attack described by officials as the largest in recent months.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and JOANNA KOZLOWSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A makeshift memorial grows in Ukraine’s capital after 1,000 days of war
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Before Russia’s invasion, this was an ordinary green lawn in the heart of Ukraine’s capital. Tourists would visit to take photos, and locals would stroll there on weekends.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senegal votes in election that will decide if president can carry out the reforms he promised
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Polls closed in Senegal on Sunday for a parliamentary election set to determine whether the country’s newly elected president can carry out ambitious reforms.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia launched more than 200 missiles, drones at Ukrainian infrastructure: Zelensky
item.description: On Sunday, Russia launched a drone and missile attack on Ukraine in what’s being seen as the largest barrage in recent months, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: British prime minister says he has no plans to talk with Putin as he reaffirms support for Ukraine
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday that he has no plan to speak with Russian President Vladimir Putin as he pledged support for Ukraine as the U.K.’s top priority at this week’s G20 summit.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wreckage of World War II warship found 80 years later
item.description: The USS Edsall, a World War II American warship, was bombed and sank three months after the attack on Pearl Harbor.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Fire kills at least 10 people in Spanish retirement home
item.description: A fire that broke out in a retirement home in the Spanish town of Villafranca de Ebro has left 10 people dead; all of the victims were reportedly elderly people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democratic senators call for federal probe into Musk's contact with Russia
item.description: A pair of Democratic senators called for a federal investigation into Elon Musk’s involvement in SpaceX’s federal contracts following reports the tech billionaire has been in regular contact with Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Protesters storm Putin-backed parliament in Georgia breakaway region
item.description: Protesters in the separatist region of Abkhazia, Georgia stormed the parliament building to demand the region's president step down and reject an investment agreement that could benefit wealthy Russians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scientists in Argentina unearth oldest tadpole fossil ever discovered
item.description: A search for dinosaur fossils unintentionally shifted into a discovery of the oldest tadpole ever discovered when a team exploring in Argentina came across the find.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Priest stabbed in the face during Mass as religion-based hate crime is on the rise worldwide
item.description: A priest stabbed in the face during Mass is one of the most recent cases of a religion-based hate crime, which is one the rise around the globe, though Jews are most often victims of the attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alex Soros, Jared Kushner reportedly obsessed with Albania
item.description: Alex Soros, whose father is Hungarian, reportedly travels to the capital Tirana so often he has called it “my second home.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Republican introduces bill to revoke normal trade relations with China
item.description: The top Republican focused on U.S. competition with China introduced legislation on Thursday to end normal trade relations with Beijing. Rep.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Democrats call for Biden to sanction 'radical' Israeli government ministers
item.description: Democrats in the House and Senate are calling on President Biden to sanction two controversial Israeli ministers, saying the two men have promoted violence against Palestinians and are pushing for annexation of territory that undermines U.S. efforts to support a two-state solution.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Iran military heads vow 'crushing' response to Israel as UN atomic chief says nuke sites shouldn't be attacked
item.description: The U.N. atomic watchdog has urged Israel not to target Iran's nuclear facilities as it continues to carry out strikes in Lebanon, Gaza and Syria, as Iran vows a "crushing" response to Jerusalem's attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Moo Deng, Thailand's baby pygmy hippo, gets official song in 4 languages
item.description: In case you can’t get enough of the little pygmy hippo Moo Deng from Thailand, there's now an official song featuring the internet's favorite baby animal — released in four languages for her global fans.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's Rubio pick divides Republicans
item.description: Tensions are brewing over President-elect Trump’s move to tap Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.) to serve as secretary of State, with some of the president-elect’s allies voicing concerns about the pick. The choice has already set into motion the process of Florida Gov.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, The Hill

item.title: UN watchdog warns time to 'maneuver' on Iran's nuclear program is shrinking: report
item.description: Rafael Grossi, head of the International Atomic Energy Agency, the U.N. nuclear watchdog, says the window of opportunity to diplomatically engage with Iran is beginning to "shrink.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US government worker charged with leaking classified documents on Israel's plans to strike Iran
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A man who worked for the U.S. government has been charged with leaking classified information assessing Israel's earlier plans to attack Iran, according to court papers filed Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 1 dead, Supreme Court evacuated as explosions rattle Brazilian capital
item.description: The Brazilian Supreme Court was evacuated Wednesday following two explosions in its vicinity, stoking security concerns in the G20's leadup.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Spain battered by new storm onslaught just weeks after deadly floods
item.description: New storms triggered school closures and transit disruptions in Spain on Wednesday, just two weeks after flash floods left over 220 people dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden, Xi to meet on Saturday in Peru, US officials say
item.description: U.S. President Joe Biden and Chinese President Xi Jinping will meet on Saturday in Peru; they are expected to hold talks 'spanning a range of global hot spots.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tourists stranded in Bali for days after Indonesia's volcanic eruption grounds international flights
item.description: Thousands of people were stranded at airports in Indonesia and Australia due to the eruption of Indonesia’s Mount Lewotobi Laki Laki volcano. media reports say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US warns Russia potentially aiding North Korea's nuclear program in direct threat to Europe, Asia
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken warns that North Korea is benefiting from aiding Russia in its war against Ukraine by potentially aiding Pyongyang's nuclear development, posing a threat for Europe and Asia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Driver rams his car into crowd in China, killing 35. Police say he was upset about his divorce
item.description: ZHUHAI, China (AP) — A man who authorities said was upset over his divorce settlement rammed his car into a crowd of people exercising at a sports complex in southern China, killing 35 and severely injuring dozens of others, police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: NG HAN GUAN and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New search for MH370 Malaysia Airlines flight could begin soon
item.description: Flight MH370 vanished on March 8, 2014, shortly after departing Kuala Lumpur for Beijing with 239 people aboard.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Church of England head Justin Welby resigns over handling of sex abuse scandal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Archbishop of Canterbury Justin Welby, head of the Church of England and spiritual leader of the global Anglican Communion, resigned Tuesday after an investigation found that he failed to tell police about serial physical and sexual abuse by a volunteer at Christian summer camps as soo
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: President Joe Biden meets with Israel's Isaac Herzog
item.description: Joe Biden met with Israel President Isaac Herzog at the White House on Tuesday, which included a gift from Herzog to Biden.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation
